rulings.Case Title: ANDREW DIMITRIOU VS. CIT FINANCIAL SERVICES INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 20, 2011, line 4, CROSS DEFENDANT ANDREW DIMITRIOU DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT - Cross-defendant's demurrer to the First Amended Answer to the Cross Complaint is overruled. No useful purpose is served by the demurrer. While the demurring party may be technically correct as to certain aspects of the answer, the better and more cost-effective approach is to seek the information that is claimed to be lacking through discovery. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 20, 2011, line 10, PLAINTIFF FOUNDERS REALTY, INC., GRINSELL, RAYMOND, TRUSTEE OF EQUITY GROWTH ASSET MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Denied without prejudice to refiling the motion with proper supporting papers. rulings.Case Title: SPRINT TELEPHONY PCS, L.P. et al VS. STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 20, 2011, line 18, DEFENDANT COUNTY OF CONTRA COSTA, COUNTY OF INYO, COUNTY OF KERN, COUNTY OF NAPA, COUNTY OF RIVERSIDE, COUNTY OF SISKIYOU DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Overruled with ten days to answer. Because a literal reading of 5148 (f) and (g) favors plaintiffs and such reading is neither nonsensical nor precluded by an appellate authority and defendants have neither shown or suggested any prejudice to them as a result of the yes box not being checked, subsections (f) and (g) are inapplicable to this case since plaintiffs¿ petition did not check the yes box on the petition. The argument made by some counties based on the SBE Assessment Manual lacks merit because the six month time limit for bringing a lawsuit does not apply to a case filed under section 5148. The jurisdiction/venue argument made some counties lacks merit because this court has jurisdiction and is a proper venue for this action. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.

Because a literal reading of 5148 (f) and (g) favors plaintiffs and such reading is neither nonsensical nor precluded by an appellate authority and defendants have neither shown or suggested any prejudice to them as a result of the yes box not being checked, subsections (f) and (g) are inapplicable to this case since plaintiffs¿ petition did not check the yes box on the petition. The argument made by some counties based on the SBE Assessment Manual lacks merit because the six month time limit for bringing a lawsuit does not apply to a case filed under section 5148. While the filing of the separate action was erroneous under CCP 1292.6, petitioner Lappano did not violate CCP 128.7. rulings.Case Title: FINISAR CORPORATION VS. JDS UNIPHASE CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay; Appendix
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 20, 2011, Line 20, RESPONDENT JDS UNIPHASE CORPORATION Motion To Stay. Denied. The Court exercises its discretion to deny the requested stay on the following grounds: the California action was filed first and it should go forward despite the later filed out of state federal action; and the motion to vacate the arbitration award is denied. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FINISAR CORPORATION VS. JDS UNIPHASE CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 20, 2011, Line 20, Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. The petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. An arbitrator¿s failure to apply the principles of res judicata or collateral estoppel properly is not a basis to vacate an arbitration award. (See Durand v. Wilshire Ins. Co. (1969) 270 Cal.App.2d 58, 60; Interinsurance Exchange of CA v. Bailes (1963) 219 Cal.App.2d 830, 836.) The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ECONOHOMES, LLC

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 20, 2011, Line 22, PLAINTIFF MELANIE GRAYSMITH'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The Demurrer to the answer is is overruled. No useful purpose is served by the demurrer. rulings.Case Title: FALKO STRUNZ et al VS. CATHEDRAL HILL ASSOCIATES, LP et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 20, 2011, Line 23, DEFENDANT CATHEDRAL HILL ASSOCIATES, LP DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with twenty days leave to amend to file an amended complaint that, as to and within each cause action alleged, the plaintiffs allege specific facts supporting each element of that cause of action. The manner in which the complaint is now written makes it impossible to know what alleged facts purportedly apply to which cause of action and each element of that cause of action. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DOMINIC GALU et al VS. GUILLERMO REY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 20, 2011, Line 24, DEFENDANT GUILLERMO REY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled in its entirety. There is no requirement in § 37.3(d)(2)(A) that a landlord request arbitration when the grounds for the increase is CC § 1954.53(d)(2). Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to support all causes of action asserted in the complaint. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.
